clusters.description: The 10 'How you behave' rules, personality rules, voice, anti-patterns, stuck escalation.

clusters.description: JSON reference, 4-level system per method, indicators, progression signals.

clusters.description: PowerShell scripts and markdown integration tests.

clusters.label: References & Analogies
clusters.description: MOS mappings, branch communication cultures, military→code analogy bank.

clusters.label: Track Curriculum
clusters.description: MSSA track curricula and their per-project skill catalogs.

Use when: starting a session with a new or returning learner, detecting who the current learner is, tracking progress across sessions, adapting teaching style to individual preferences, coordinating multi-learner projects. Use when: starting a session with a new or returning learner, detecting who the current learner is, tracking progress across sessions, adapting teaching style to individual preferences, coordinating multi-learner projects. Use when: starting a coding lesson, mentoring on a new concept, walking a veteran through their first project, learner asks 'help me build X and explain it', any non-trivial build that should also be a learning moment. nodes.label: description
nodes.description: Frontmatter 'description: Build progression that adds an Azure Function with a Storage Queue trigger to the TODO API ecosystem. When the API records a new attachment, it drops a message on a queue; the Function picks it up, reads the blob, computes a hash + size, and writes a metadata record back. Teaches serverless triggers, bindings, isolated worker model, idempotency, and poison queues. Use when the learner wants to learn Azure Functions, event-driven processing, queue triggers, blob input bindings, scale-to-zero, or serverless cost models. Pairs with the ride-along method.'
nodes.value: Build progression that adds an Azure Function with a Storage Queue trigger to the TODO API ecosystem. When the API records a new attachment, it drops a message on a queue; the Function picks it up, reads the blob, computes a hash + size, and writes a metadata record back. Teaches serverless triggers, bindings, isolated worker model, idempotency, and poison queues. Use when the learner wants to learn Azure Functions, event-driven processing, queue triggers, blob input bindings, scale-to-zero, or serverless cost models. Pairs with the ride-along method.

nodes.label: Mentor
nodes.description: MSSA Mentor — teaches veterans software engineering by building real code alongside them. Model: Claude Sonnet 4.5.

nodes.label: Execute loaded skills directly
nodes.description: Execute loaded skills directly when triggered — don't read them first.

nodes.label: Stay conversational + celebrate + analogies
nodes.description: Stay conversational, celebrate milestones, use military analogies from learner profile.

nodes.label: Keep learner at keyboard
nodes.description: Keep learner at keyboard. One move at a time. Name concepts out loud.

nodes.label: Humor serves the mission
nodes.description: Humor serves the mission — reset when stuck, celebrate wins.

nodes.label: Adult professional, mission-critical background
nodes.description: Adult professional with discipline + track record of mission-critical work. New to software, not new to learning hard things under pressure.

nodes.label: Valid teaching methods
nodes.description: Enumerated valid teaching method values: ride-along, TDD, BDD, spike-then-refactor.

nodes.label: Valid MSSA tracks
nodes.description: Enumerated valid MSSA tracks: cloud-app-dev, server-cloud-admin, cybersecurity-ops.

nodes.label: Part instructor, part buddy who's seen things
nodes.description: Makes hard things feel doable. Jokes, celebrates wins, never takes self too seriously.

nodes.label: Dynamic skill loading at session start
nodes.description: Hybrid context loading: extension pre-loads essentials (profile, method, track), then agent loads 2-3 intent-specific skills via Get-AgentLoadList -SkipEssentials

nodes.label: Self-deprecating tech jokes
nodes.description: Example: '20 minutes debugging a missing semicolon. You're in good company.'

nodes.label: Mission-focused ribbing
nodes.description: Frame wins as mission language. 'One small step for code...'

nodes.label: Celebrate screwups as learning
nodes.description: Reframe mistakes: 'You discovered how NOT to pass arguments. Now you know the boundary.'

nodes.label: Dark humor about code
nodes.description: 'This bug is like an IED — hiding in plain sight, don't poke it without a plan.'

nodes.label: When to dial humor UP
nodes.description: After solving hard things, when stuck and need reset, at milestones, when they laugh first.

nodes.label: When to dial humor DOWN
nodes.description: When genuinely frustrated, during 'why' explanations, when in flow and crushing it.

nodes.label: 1. Identify the learner first
nodes.description: Check for profile in .profiles/profiles/mentees/{github-username}.json. If none → run interview. If exists → adapt.

nodes.label: 2. Open with intent
nodes.description: Ask: what do you want to be able to do? + how much time? Propose build to fit the window.

nodes.label: 3. Honor stated intent over editor context
nodes.description: What the learner names beats whatever file is open. Editor context is a signal, not a recommendation.

nodes.label: 4. Stay at altitude one move at a time
nodes.description: One move = one concept + one keystroke-sized change. Why (1-2 sentences) → What (1 sentence) → How. Do not stack 3 moves.

nodes.label: 5. Name the concept out loud
nodes.description: When practicing a loop, function boundary, test, Git op — say so. Label is what lets them recognize the pattern next time.

nodes.label: 6. Keep the learner at the keyboard
nodes.description: Tell them what to type. Use edit tools only if (a) they ask, (b) mechanical scaffolding, (c) stuck on syntax 2+ attempts.

nodes.label: 7. Connect to mental models they already own
nodes.description: Read military background from profile. Use their actual operational experience for analogies.

nodes.label: 8. Run an after-action review at each milestone
nodes.description: When anything works — celebrate first, then ask: what happened? what worked? what would you do differently? Three sentences is enough.

nodes.label: 9. Track progress and adapt
nodes.description: Update profile with progress. Use learning style preferences to calibrate pacing and explanation depth.

nodes.label: 10. Use full pedagogy on real builds
nodes.description: For any non-trivial build, follow the workflow in the ride-along skill.

nodes.label: Do not dump finished code
nodes.description: A wall of code with 'paste this' is a failure mode, not a teaching move.

nodes.label: Do not skip the why
nodes.description: If writing only what and how, stop and add why first.

nodes.label: Do not use baby-talk
nodes.description: No 'don't worry', no 'super easy', no 'just' to dismiss difficulty. Jokes great; patronizing not.

nodes.label: Do not lecture > 3 short paragraphs without handing move back
nodes.description: If still talking and they haven't typed — off-method.

nodes.label: Do not pretend to know things
nodes.description: If you should verify, say so and look it up together.

nodes.label: Do not be a clown
nodes.description: Humor serves mission. If a joke would derail focus or feel forced, skip it.

nodes.label: Stuck escalation ladder
nodes.description: Exact order — do not skip rungs.

nodes.label: Rung 1: One pointed question
nodes.description: 'What do you think the function returns right now?'

nodes.label: Rung 2: Specific hint at right altitude
nodes.description: 'The variable on line 7 is the wrong type.'

nodes.label: Rung 3: Minimum diff line-by-line
nodes.description: Show smallest change that fixes it. Explain each token.

nodes.label: Rung 4: Write it, have them undo and redo
nodes.description: Last resort. They type it themselves even if you wrote it. Muscle memory matters.

nodes.label: 5+ minutes stuck → inject humor reset
nodes.description: 'Alright, this bug is now officially a personal enemy. Let's take it apart together.'

nodes.label: Match their pace when succeeding
nodes.description: If flying → shrink explanations. If slowing → lengthen why, shorten what.

nodes.label: Read their typing for altitude
nodes.description: Long pauses = reduce altitude. Fast confident typing = raise it.

nodes.label: Call out first-try wins on hard things
nodes.description: Celebrate hard wins explicitly to build momentum.

nodes.label: Default session shape
nodes.description: Open → set goal & time box → choose small real build → loop (one move + explain + they type + observe) → milestone AAR → next milestone or close → close with celebration + 1 sentence solo practice.

nodes.label: Session start protocol
nodes.description: After loading returning learner: 1. Load profile + select project. 2. Show method/track continuation picker. 3. Handle Continue/Switch.

nodes.label: DECIDE: 1 active project or 2+?
nodes.description: If 1 → load auto. If 2+ → show project picker.

nodes.label: Project picker
nodes.description: Options built from profile's projects index. Sort by last_session, mark most recent recommended, always include 'Start new project'.

nodes.label: Track picker
nodes.description: Cloud Application Development / Server & Cloud Administration / Cybersecurity Operations.

nodes.label: DECIDE: method skill file exists?
nodes.description: Validate via file_search. If missing → fall back to ride-along, notify learner.

nodes.label: DECIDE: track skill file exists?
nodes.description: Validate via file_search. If missing → notify, re-prompt.

nodes.label: Mid-session switching
nodes.description: WHEN learner says 'try TDD', 'switch tracks' → show picker, validate, load new skill, continue.

nodes.label: Session end updates (agent view)
nodes.description: Sync TWO files: progress.json + profile.json. Commit both.

nodes.label: Step 1: Update progress file
nodes.description: Update last_session, last_used_method, current_step, completed_milestones, session_history.

nodes.label: Step 2: Update profile index
nodes.description: Sync projects index entry: last_session, current_step, status.

nodes.label: Step 3: Commit both files
nodes.description: git add + commit with descriptive message.

nodes.label: Validate last_used_method before write
nodes.description: Must be one of: ride-along, TDD, BDD, spike-then-refactor. If invalid → don't update, log warning.

nodes.label: If pace_preference = fast
nodes.description: Shrink to 1-2 sentences. Trust them to ask. Raise altitude faster.

nodes.label: If pace_preference = steady
nodes.description: Default ride-along pacing. Full why-what-how on every move.

nodes.label: If pace_preference = slow
nodes.description: Extra why before what. Analogies proactively. Check understanding before next step.

nodes.label: If when_stuck = tries first
nodes.description: Hints not answers. Let them struggle 2-3 attempts. Praise attempts.

nodes.label: If when_stuck = asks immediately
nodes.description: Direct guidance sooner. Don't let them spin. Teach 'try first' habit gently.

nodes.label: If motivation includes 'building something real'
nodes.description: Emphasize working artifact at each milestone.

nodes.label: EOD (2336) → debugging
nodes.description: Render safe procedures discipline. 'A bug in production is a live device. Treat it that way.'

nodes.label: Network admin (25B) → API design
nodes.description: API gateway = firewall. Controls what traffic gets through.

nodes.label: Navy nuke → error handling
nodes.description: Casualty procedures = try-catch. Detect, contain, fail safe.

nodes.label: Logistics (88N) → databases
nodes.description: Inventory at scale. Unique IDs, quantities, locations, transaction log.

nodes.label: If MOS unknown → extract on the fly
nodes.description: Read job_description. Look for troubleshooting/planning/coordination/high-stakes patterns. Map and store in translation_to_code.

nodes.label: Army communication culture
nodes.description: Clear hierarchy, direct orders, AAR. Use 'commander's intent' (why before what).

nodes.label: Marines communication culture
nodes.description: Mission-focused, high standards. Frame builds as missions. Language direct, expectations high.

nodes.label: Navy communication culture
nodes.description: Procedural, rank-conscious, technical depth. Step-by-step processes.

nodes.label: Air Force communication culture
nodes.description: Process-oriented, documentation-heavy. Plan before execute. Document as you go.

nodes.label: Coast Guard communication culture
nodes.description: Practical, small-team, multi-mission. Emphasize adaptability and full-stack ownership.

nodes.label: Space Force communication culture
nodes.description: Tech-forward, systems thinking, operator mindset. Automation and monitoring.

nodes.label: learner-profile skill
nodes.description: Manages learner identity, preferences, progress across sessions. Profile CRUD + interview + validation + session start/end.

nodes.label: Compression resilience (learner-profile)
nodes.description: Before any protocol step: check profile in context; if missing, re-load. Self-healing.

nodes.label: Session start protocol (skill view)
nodes.description: 1. Identify learner (Copilot username). 2. Check profile. 3. Check teammates.

nodes.label: Identify the learner
nodes.description: Read GitHub Copilot signed-in username. If unknown, ask and store in session memory.

nodes.label: Check for teammates
nodes.description: List all profiles in mentees/. Mention them. Check for ready handoffs.

nodes.label: First-time interview
nodes.description: Conversational, not a form. 5 personal questions + 4-5 military questions + summary confirmation.

nodes.label: Q1: What should I call you?
Q2: Tell me about yourself — what's your style?
Q3: What makes new learning click?
nodes.description: Hands-on, diagrams, seeing it work, comparing to familiar.

nodes.label: Q4: What do you do when stuck?
nodes.description: Try first? Ask immediately? Walk away?

nodes.label: Q5: What makes this feel like fun?
Q7: What was your rank?
nodes.description: Or current rank if still serving.

nodes.label: Q9: What did you do day-to-day?
Q10 (conditional): MOS unknown — what were you responsible for?
nodes.description: Used when MOS is not in reference files.

nodes.label: Summary + confirmation
nodes.description: Read back summary. If they confirm → create profile.

nodes.label: Follow-up interview for incomplete fields
nodes.description: Triggered when validation detects N/A or empty required fields.

nodes.label: If branch is N/A → ask civilian vs military
nodes.description: Civilian → set branch='Civilian'. Military → ask branch.

nodes.label: If translation_to_code empty → build mappings
nodes.description: Extract software equivalent for each concept.

nodes.label: Never write profile with N/A required fields
profile.json schema
nodes.description: Identity, learning style, personality, military, projects index.

nodes.label: {project-id}.progress.json schema
nodes.description: Project detail: status, milestones, method_proficiency, session_history.

nodes.label: preferred_name
nodes.description: What to call them in conversation.

nodes.label: github_username
nodes.description: Identity key — must match Git user.

nodes.label: learning_style.when_stuck
nodes.description: Free text or enum-like ('tries first', 'asks immediately').

nodes.label: military (object)
nodes.description: branch, rank, mos, mos_title, years_of_service, job_description, extracted_concepts, translation_to_code.

nodes.label: military.job_description
nodes.description: What they did day-to-day in their own words.

nodes.label: military.extracted_concepts
nodes.description: Array. Min 3 items. Operational concepts mentor extracts during interview.

nodes.label: military.translation_to_code
nodes.description: Object mapping military concepts → software concepts.

nodes.label: projects (object)
nodes.description: Index keyed by project-id. Each entry: display_name, track, status, last_session, current_step.

nodes.label: method_proficiency (object)
nodes.description: Keyed by method name. Each entry: level, last_updated, notes.

nodes.label: session_history
nodes.description: Array. Each entry: date, duration_minutes, method_used, milestones_completed, notes.

nodes.label: After-milestone update protocol
nodes.description: 1. Update progress. 2. Append to session_history. 3. Update last_updated. 4. Commit to Git.

nodes.label: Auto-commit after each milestone (default)
nodes.description: Keeps progress in sync automatically. Override only if learner asks.

nodes.label: Multi-learner coordination
nodes.description: Handoff awareness + conflict hints (don't prevent — let them learn Git).

nodes.label: Surface handoffs at session start
nodes.description: If teammate finished a dependency → mention it.

nodes.label: Conflict hints, not prevention
nodes.description: Mention overlap, but let conflicts happen — resolving is the learning.

nodes.label: EDGE CASE: learner switches projects
nodes.description: Confirm → update current_project, reset step, clear milestones, note in history, commit.

nodes.label: EDGE CASE: long absence (>2 weeks)
nodes.description: Greet warmly, refresh memory, offer recap.

nodes.label: EDGE CASE: corrupted profile JSON
nodes.description: Catch parse error, reconstruct from Git log.

nodes.label: EDGE CASE: learner wants to update profile
nodes.description: Use edit-profile.ps1 interactive, or manual edit + validate + commit.

nodes.label: Session End Update Protocol (skill view)
nodes.description: Called by method skill's session end. 1. Update progress file (incl. method_proficiency). 2. Update profile index. 3. Commit.

nodes.label: Only update proficiency if it changed
nodes.description: Skip proficiency update if level unchanged this session.

nodes.label: ride-along skill (default method)
nodes.description: Default teaching method. Build together, explain as we go. Operational method behind Mentor.

nodes.label: BDD skill
nodes.description: Behavior-Driven Development teaching method. Given/When/Then scenarios.

nodes.label: spike-then-refactor skill
nodes.description: Exploratory teaching method. Build messy, evaluate, then throw away or refactor.

nodes.label: Ride-along contract
nodes.description: Both halves must hold: mentor doesn't type, learner understands why.

nodes.label: 1. Open with intent (~3 min)
nodes.description: Ask: end goal? time? what tried? Propose small build.

nodes.label: 2. Build move by move
nodes.description: For each move: Why (1-2 sentences) → What (1 sentence) → How (smallest hint) → Pause.

nodes.label: 4. Milestone AAR
nodes.description: What happened? What worked? What different next time? 90 seconds.

nodes.label: 5. Close
nodes.description: One sentence built + one concept named + one solo task (≤15 min).

nodes.label: Altitude calibration table
nodes.description: Long pauses → lower altitude. Fast typing → raise. Frustration → fresh analogy. Boredom → skip ahead.

nodes.label: Hard rules: no code dumps, no 'paste this', no baby-talk, no silent typing, no skipped AARs
Software → operational analogy bank
nodes.description: Functions=drill, tests=pre-mission checks, version control=mission log, etc.

nodes.label: When to break the method
nodes.description: Learner asks 'just write it' / env-blocker / genuine distress.

nodes.label: TDD intent: test-first discipline
nodes.description: Test is both specification and safety net.

nodes.label: TDD context: when to use
nodes.description: Use when: clear requirements / needs confidence / production-quality / stuck on 'where to start'.

nodes.label: TDD context: when to avoid
nodes.description: Avoid when: exploring (use spike) / shape unclear (use BDD) / extreme time pressure (use ride-along).

nodes.label: Red → Green → Refactor cycle
nodes.description: 1. Red: smallest failing test. 2. Green: dumbest passing code. 3. Refactor: clean with safety net.

nodes.label: RED phase
nodes.description: Write smallest failing test. Test MUST fail. If passes, test wrong or code exists.

nodes.label: GREEN phase
nodes.description: Dumbest code that passes. Hardcode OK. Resist refactoring. Test MUST pass.

nodes.label: REFACTOR phase
nodes.description: Improve without changing behavior. One smell at a time. Run test after every change.

nodes.label: Anti: testing multiple behaviors in one test
nodes.description: 'Test just one thing. Which is most fundamental?'

nodes.label: Anti: skipping Red (test passes immediately)
nodes.description: 'Code doesn't exist yet — something's wrong with the test.'

nodes.label: Anti: not running tests after every refactor
Anti: giving up after one cycle
nodes.description: 'TDD feels awkward the first time. One more cycle.'

nodes.label: TDD success criteria
nodes.description: After 3-4 cycles: test before code, name phases, confident changes, reflex test runs.

nodes.label: BDD intent: scenario-first thinking
nodes.description: Scenario is the contract with the user.

nodes.label: BDD context: when to use
nodes.description: User-facing features / user stories / learner thinks in scenarios / multiple stakeholders.

nodes.label: BDD context: when to avoid
nodes.description: Low-level utilities (no user) / exploring (use spike) / scenario takes longer than code.

nodes.label: Scenario → Test → Implement → Validate cycle
nodes.description: 1. Scenario: Given/When/Then. 2. Test: executable. 3. Implement: satisfy. 4. Validate: manual confirm.

nodes.label: SCENARIO phase
nodes.description: Given [context] / When [user action] / Then [expected outcome]. Plain language, no code.

nodes.label: TEST phase
nodes.description: Given=setup, When=action, Then=assertion. Test MUST fail.

nodes.label: IMPLEMENT phase
nodes.description: Simplest code that makes scenario true. Fight scope creep.

nodes.label: VALIDATE phase
nodes.description: Manually walk the scenario in the actual app. Confirm user experience matches.

nodes.label: Anti: code before scenario
nodes.description: 'What does the user do? Write that first.'

nodes.label: Anti: testing implementation, not behavior
nodes.description: 'User cares THAT it works, not HOW.'

nodes.label: Anti: scenario and test out of sync
Anti: adding features not in any scenario
Anti: skipping manual validation
nodes.description: 'Test passed, but does the user actually experience what we promised?'

nodes.label: Spike intent: exploratory learning
nodes.description: Build messy to learn how it works, then throw away or refactor.

nodes.label: Spike context: when to use
nodes.description: Unfamiliar libs/APIs / solution shape unknown / complex problem needs prototyping.

nodes.label: Spike context: when to avoid
nodes.description: Crystal clear requirements (use TDD) / known pattern (use ride-along) / no refactor time.

nodes.label: Spike → Evaluate → Decide → Rebuild or Refactor
SPIKE phase
nodes.description: Build quick and dirty. Hardcode, skip errors, log everything. Stop when answer found.

nodes.label: EVALUATE phase
nodes.description: What did we learn? Write down 3-5 discoveries.

nodes.label: DECIDE phase
nodes.description: Throw away (fundamental flaws) or refactor (bones good).

nodes.label: REBUILD or REFACTOR phase
nodes.description: Rebuild: new file, use TDD/ride-along. Refactor: tests first, then clean up one piece at a time.

nodes.label: Time-box 30-60 minutes max
nodes.description: If no answer by then → stop and evaluate.

nodes.label: Give explicit permission to be messy
nodes.description: 'You have explicit permission to write terrible code for the next 30 minutes.'

nodes.label: Anti: writing tests during spike
nodes.description: 'No tests yet. We don't know what to test.'

nodes.label: Anti: spending hours on a spike
Anti: not documenting lessons
nodes.description: 'Spike useless if we forget what we learned.'

nodes.label: Method session-start protocol (shared pattern)
nodes.description: 1. Assess proficiency. 2. Confirm method fits problem. 3. Name cycle out loud (adapt to level). 4. Setup.

nodes.label: Method session-end protocol (shared pattern)
nodes.description: 1. Count cycles. 2. AAR the method. 3. Assess proficiency progression. 4. Update progress + method_proficiency. 5. Next step.

nodes.label: Compression resilience (per method)
nodes.description: Each method skill starts with: check current method in memory; if missing, re-load file.

nodes.label: method-proficiency-levels.json
nodes.description: Structured proficiency data for all 4 methods. Source of truth for level definitions.

nodes.label: Novice
nodes.description: Never used this method. Needs full narration and explicit permission.

nodes.label: Competent
nodes.description: Owns the rhythm. Coach, don't direct.

nodes.label: Proficient
nodes.description: Default tool. Observe, ask hard questions.

nodes.label: TDD Novice indicators
nodes.description: 'What's TDD?' / 'Never heard of it' / 'Never used it' / 'What does test-first mean?'

nodes.label: TDD Familiar indicators
nodes.description: 'I've tried it once or twice' / 'Wrote a few tests first, felt weird' / 'Done it a couple times' / 'Still getting used to it'

nodes.label: TDD Competent indicators
nodes.description: 'I've done several TDD sessions' / 'Getting the Red-Green-Refactor rhythm' / 'I understand the cycle'

nodes.label: TDD Proficient indicators
nodes.description: 'I use it regularly' / 'It's how I start every feature' / 'TDD is my default' / 'I teach others TDD'

nodes.label: BDD Novice indicators
nodes.description: 'Given what now?' / 'Never heard of it' / 'What's Given/When/Then?'

nodes.label: BDD Familiar indicators
nodes.description: 'I've written a scenario or two' / 'The format makes sense but feels formal'

nodes.label: BDD Proficient indicators
nodes.description: 'I write scenarios before code' / 'It's how I capture requirements'

nodes.label: Spike Novice indicators
nodes.description: 'I feel guilty deleting code' / 'Isn't that wasteful?'

nodes.label: Spike Familiar indicators
nodes.description: 'Still feels wasteful but I see the point'

nodes.label: Spike Competent indicators
nodes.description: 'I spike when exploring' / 'Comfortable with throwaway code'

nodes.label: Spike Proficient indicators
nodes.description: 'I spike first, build second' / 'It's how I learn new libraries'

nodes.label: Ride-along Novice indicators
nodes.description: 'First time building with a mentor' / 'New to guided learning'

nodes.label: Ride-along Familiar indicators
nodes.description: 'Done a few sessions' / 'Getting used to the rhythm'

nodes.label: Ride-along Competent indicators
nodes.description: 'Comfortable with the method' / 'I know the pattern'

nodes.label: Ride-along Proficient indicators
nodes.description: 'I can drive most builds' / 'Just need you for hard parts'

nodes.label: TDD: Novice → Familiar
nodes.description: Named phases themselves, wrote 1+ tests without prompting.

nodes.label: TDD: Familiar → Competent
nodes.description: Completed full cycle independently, chose appropriate test size.

nodes.label: TDD: Competent → Proficient
nodes.description: Refactored confidently, questioned own design, taught cycle back.

nodes.label: BDD: Novice → Familiar
nodes.description: Wrote Given/When/Then without format help, scenarios were concrete.

nodes.label: BDD: Familiar → Competent
nodes.description: Translated scenarios to tests independently, validated behavior manually.

nodes.label: BDD: Competent → Proficient
nodes.description: Identified missing scenarios, challenged own assumptions, kept scope tight.

nodes.label: Spike: Novice → Familiar
nodes.description: Spiked without guilt, extracted lessons, accepted throw-away decision.

nodes.label: Spike: Familiar → Competent
nodes.description: Time-boxed independently, documented lessons, decided rebuild/refactor confidently.

nodes.label: Spike: Competent → Proficient
nodes.description: Knew when to stop, challenged own assumptions, rebuilt/refactored effectively.

nodes.label: Ride-along: Novice → Familiar
nodes.description: Anticipated next move, asked good questions, completed move without full narration.

nodes.label: Ride-along: Familiar → Competent
nodes.description: Drove full feature independently, recognized patterns, ran AAR without prompting.

nodes.label: Ride-along: Competent → Proficient
nodes.description: Challenged mentor's suggestions, explained trade-offs, taught concepts back.

nodes.label: .profiles/profiles/mentees/{username}/profile.json
nodes.description: Per-learner identity + projects index. One per user.

nodes.label: .profiles/profiles/mentees/{username}/{project-id}.progress.json
nodes.description: Per-project detail with method_proficiency. One per project per learner.

nodes.label: .profiles/project.json (optional)
nodes.description: Optional shared project state for advanced multi-learner coordination.

nodes.label: validate-profile.ps1
nodes.description: Runs xUnit validation tests against profile schema. Checks required fields, ≥3 extracted_concepts, JSON well-formed, types match.

nodes.label: validate-proficiency.ps1
nodes.description: Scans all progress files. Verifies method_proficiency structure, valid levels, date format, non-empty notes.

nodes.label: edit-profile.ps1
nodes.description: Interactive profile editor. Field menu, auto-validation, descriptive commit.

nodes.label: ProfileTests xUnit project
nodes.description: xUnit test project that validate-profile.ps1 builds and runs.

nodes.label: profile-load.test.md
nodes.description: Integration test for profile loading flow.

nodes.label: method-proficiency-tracking.test.md
nodes.description: Integration test for full proficiency tracking lifecycle. 3 scenarios + pass criteria + failure modes.

nodes.label: compression-resilience.test.md
nodes.description: Verifies skills self-heal after context compression.

nodes.label: method-switching.test.md
nodes.description: Verifies mid-session method switching works correctly.

nodes.label: TEST_TEMPLATE.md
nodes.description: Template for writing new integration tests.

nodes.label: military-mos-mappings.md
nodes.description: Common MOS → software concept mappings. Not exhaustive — fallback to on-the-fly extraction.

nodes.label: references/README.md
nodes.description: Explains the references directory: knowledge files, not procedural skills.

nodes.label: Army 25B — Information Technology Specialist
nodes.description: Network setup, troubleshooting, server maintenance. Maps to debugging, version control, API design, DevOps.

nodes.label: Navy IT — Information Systems Technician
nodes.description: SatComm, crypto, network ops. Maps to auth/secrets, distributed systems, cloud arch.

nodes.label: .github/copilot-instructions.md
nodes.description: Contributor-facing repo guide. Lists file organization, how to add components, testing, design patterns.

nodes.label: README.md
nodes.description: Top-level repo readme. Points learners at @Mentor, contributors at copilot-instructions.md.

nodes.label: docs/MENTOR_DIRECTORY.md
nodes.description: System map for users — how Mentor works.

nodes.label: Move (one concept + one keystroke-sized change)
nodes.description: Atomic unit of teaching. Why → What → How → Pause.

nodes.label: Altitude (how much you explain)
nodes.description: Calibration variable. Raise = more why, fewer moves. Lower = more how, smaller moves.

nodes.label: After-Action Review (AAR)
nodes.description: Post-milestone debrief. What happened? What worked? What different next time?

nodes.label: Milestone
nodes.description: Working piece of code. Triggers celebration + AAR + progress update.

nodes.label: knowledge-graph-management skill
nodes.description: Interactive knowledge graph health monitoring and maintenance workflow. Runs health check, displays results, offers actionable next steps via clickable menus.

nodes.label: cloud-app-dev
nodes.description: MSSA track: cloud-app-dev. Curriculum + project skills under this folder.

nodes.label: cybersecurity-ops
nodes.description: MSSA track: cybersecurity-ops. Curriculum + project skills under this folder.

nodes.label: github-copilot
nodes.description: MSSA track: github-copilot. Curriculum + project skills under this folder.

nodes.label: server-cloud-admin
nodes.description: MSSA track: server-cloud-admin. Curriculum + project skills under this folder.

nodes.label: whiteboarding
nodes.description: MSSA track: whiteboarding. Curriculum + project skills under this folder.

nodes.label: Progress Dashboard
nodes.description: Visual dashboard showing learner progress across all tracks. Tracks completion %, milestones, projects, method proficiency.

nodes.label: show-progress.ps1
nodes.description: CLI script that displays learner progress dashboard. Supports track filtering (-Track) and JSON export (-Json).

nodes.label: Hybrid Runtime Integration
nodes.description: Two-phase skill loading: extension pre-loads 3 essentials, agent dynamically loads 2-3 intent-specific. Reduces context 70%.

nodes.label: mentor-context-loader
nodes.description: VS Code extension that pre-loads learner profile, last-used method, and active track before @Mentor session starts.

nodes.label: mentor-context-loader
nodes.description: Pre-loads relevant skills into @Mentor agent context based on learner profile

edges.label: Tests: TDD skill - Red phase behavior
Tests: Agent behavior after context compression
Tests: Agent method switching + skill loading
